metric_value: Server-based technology analogous to OLAP but applied to non-numeric information; unique in market
notes: Aberdeen describes OLAP-I as distinctive from numeric OLAP

metric_value: Analyzes and defines complex relationships between disparate information sources; dynamically reorganizes content in real-time based on user behavior
Pursuing Java and Internet/intranet/extranet-enabled markets needing more effective access formats
notes: Strategy targeting emerging web-enabled enterprise market

metric_value: Perspecta should be on the short list for applications benefiting from more effective interfaces
Aberdeen implies Perspecta is a viable market participant with a distinctive product worthy of enterprise consideration
notes: Implicit viability endorsement based on product uniqueness

metric_value: Perspecta survived until at least 2002 (added XML support) but never achieved mainstream commercial adoption; company eventually dissolved
notes: Source: xml.com 1998; company did not achieve scale needed for long-term survival

metric_value: Companies amassing digital data creating information warehouses that are dispersed and hard to manage — industry-wide problem
notes: Aberdeen frames the problem space Perspecta addresses

metric_value: Elevates user from two-dimensional information environment; extends digital metaphor to new level via dynamic content reorganization
